review_summary: The transcript and unit summary are sufficient to generate 2 reinforcement questions about EEG and the motivation for invasive BCI recording.

question_bank.question: What does EEG measure in the historical overview of BCIs?
choices: Wave-like electrical signals recorded from electrodes placed on the scalp
Blood-flow changes inside deep brain tissue
Muscle activations in the jaw and lips
Only the firing of a single neuron at a time
question_bank.explanation: EEG places electrodes on the scalp and records coarse electrical rhythms generated by large populations of neurons.

question_bank.question: Why is scalp EEG not precise enough for high-performance movement or speech decoding?
choices: It averages activity from many neurons, so fine local information is lost
It can measure only during sleep
It records signals too quickly to interpret
It works only for visual tasks
question_bank.explanation: Scalp recordings blur together the activity of many neurons, which makes them useful for broad brain-state signals but too coarse for fine-grained decoding.
